summary: A US-Iran ceasefire brokered overnight sent WTI crude tumbling nearly 19% — its sharpest drop in almost six years — after Trump agreed to suspend military action contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Markets now see the Fed on hold as the energy shock eases, but attention is shifting to whether the oil collapse feeds through into inflation data and what that means for rates.
tweet: WTI plunged 19% after Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz — the biggest single-day crude drop since 2020 — but fresh Section 301 trade probes keep tariff risk simmering under the surface.
